skill.name: receipt-scanning-tools
skill.description: Guides receipt entry, OCR setup, database checks, and troubleshooting for the nonprofit_finance_db receipt scanning tools.
skill.summary: Work with nonprofit receipt scanning tools, manual entry flows, database schema checks, and OCR configuration.
skill.author: EGAdams

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk alerts, but most are false positives from markdown code fences, SQL examples, and project documentation rather than executable skill code. Confirmed concerns are legitimate but elevated: the skill directs users to run local scripts, access project files, configure secrets, and modify finance database records. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Command and Script Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to activate a local virtual environment and run project Python scripts, including API, menu, transaction, and receipt tools. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it can execute arbitrary project code in the user environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and actionable, so the execution risk is real. Confidence is below very high because the commands are project administration steps, not hidden or malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Finance Database Mutation Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows and examples for adding merchants, saving expenses, and running a delete-expenses tool. These operations can alter or remove financial records if followed without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database write and delete workflows are directly described in the skill text. The risk is operational data integrity rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Environment and Secret Configuration References
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to use a .env file for database configuration and shows an OCR API key variable. The examples use placeholders for secrets, but users could expose credentials if they paste real values into prompts or logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env and API key references are explicit, but no real password or key value is present. This supports a medium confidence warning instead of a high severity finding.

medium_findings.title: Organization-Specific Names and Local Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill embeds absolute local paths and names of nonprofit recipients, ministries, workers, and members. Publishing this content may expose organization-specific operational details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The local paths and names are visible in the reviewed file and are specific to one environment. This is a marketplace privacy and portability concern, not proof of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static alerts labeled as Ruby or shell backtick execution come from markdown fences and inline code examples. They are documentation syntax, not executable code inside the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are markdown documentation blocks containing commands, SQL, or Python examples. They do not execute unless a user deliberately runs them.

low_findings.title: Loopback Database Host False Positive
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP address is 127.0.0.1 for a local database configuration. This is not an external network endpoint or exfiltration destination.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value is the standard loopback address and appears in a database configuration block. No external host or data transfer behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Schema Inspection False Positives
low_findings.description: Static system reconnaissance findings refer to DESCRIBE, SELECT, and COUNT examples used to inspect the project database schema. These are normal troubleshooting steps for this receipt database skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The queries are read-only inspection examples except where separate mutation workflows are already reported. The context is database troubleshooting, not host reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Not Supported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts appear to be triggered by unrelated text such as markdown metadata, SQL fields, or identifiers. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic operations in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations do not contain cryptographic code or hashing logic. The confidence is high, but not perfect because only SKILL.md exists in this package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Project Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes copyable commands that run local Python tools and project scripts. Users should review the underlying project code before running these commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and can execute local code. They are expected for this skill, so this is a warning rather than evidence of abuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database Write and Delete Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references database insert patterns and a delete-expenses script. These operations can change financial records and should require backups or confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill plainly describes database mutations. It does not show malicious intent, but the impact can be significant if used on production data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Configuration Touchpoints
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references .env database settings and a GEMINI_API_KEY variable. Secret values are placeholders, but the workflow touches sensitive configuration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential-related variable names are present and relevant to OCR and database access. No actual secret value was found.

content.user_title: Track Receipts in Nonprofit Finance Tools
content.value_statement: Nonprofit finance teams need reliable receipt entry, category checks, and OCR setup without guessing project paths. This skill provides guided workflows for receipt tools, database inspection, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Shows the project paths for the receipt scanning tools and virtual environment.
Explains the categories, merchants, expenses, and receipt metadata tables.
Guides manual receipt entry with merchant and category selection steps.
Provides commands for launching receipt tools and checking database connectivity.
Documents patterns for adding merchants, listing categories, and saving expenses.
Lists troubleshooting steps for schema, import, and database connection errors.
limitations: It is tailored to one nonprofit_finance_db project and uses hardcoded local paths.
It requires an existing Python environment, database, and project source tree.
OCR usage depends on a configured Gemini API key and supporting project code.
Image upload, bulk import, advanced merchant management, and search are listed as future work.

use_cases.title: Enter Receipts Consistently
use_cases.description: Follow the manual entry workflow to select merchants, choose categories, review details, and save expenses.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Finance Database Errors
use_cases.description: Use schema checks and connection tests to resolve missing columns, import errors, and database setup problems.

use_cases.title: Extend Receipt Tooling
use_cases.description: Follow the documented code patterns to add receipt tools, merchant helpers, and category lookup functions.

prompt_templates.title: Start Manual Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me run the manual receipt entry workflow for this nonprofit finance project. Show the steps I should follow before saving a receipt.

prompt_templates.title: Check Database Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me verify the receipt database schema and connection settings. Focus on categories, merchants, and recent expense records.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Category Query
prompt_templates.prompt: I received an unknown category_path column error. Help me inspect the actual categories table and rewrite the query safely.

prompt_templates.title: Design a New Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a new receipt scanning tool that follows the existing project patterns, validates user input, and protects finance data.

output_examples.input: I need to enter a receipt manually.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists the setup checks, launches the menu workflow, explains merchant selection, and reminds the user to review amount, date, category, and description before saving.

output_examples.input: My category query fails with an unknown column error.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains how to inspect the table, confirms the available columns, and recommends a hierarchical category query that matches the documented schema.

output_examples.input: I want to add a receipt upload feature.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines a project-aligned implementation plan covering file validation, OCR configuration, database metadata, user review, and rollback considerations.

best_practices: Review project scripts before running commands that modify financial records.
Use placeholders for secrets in prompts and keep real .env values local.
Test receipt workflows on small sample data before processing production records.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real database passwords or API keys into chat prompts.
Do not run delete or import tools on production data without a backup.
Do not assume schema fields exist before checking the actual database structure.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users run and maintain receipt scanning, manual entry, database checks, and troubleshooting workflows for one nonprofit finance project.

faq.question: Does it include working OCR code?
faq.answer: The skill documents OCR setup and project files, but OCR execution depends on the project code and a configured Gemini API key.

faq.question: Can it work with any finance database?
faq.answer: No. It is written for the nonprofit_finance_db schema and local project paths shown in the skill.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the commands directly?
faq.answer: Review the local project scripts first, especially tools that add, delete, or import financial records.

faq.question: Does the skill contain real secrets?
faq.answer: No real API key or database password was found. The skill does reference .env settings and secret variable names.

faq.question: What should be changed before broad marketplace use?
faq.answer: Replace hardcoded local paths and organization-specific names with configurable examples and generic sample data.
